payload.text:
قك باضافة ميزة رفع الصورة مع الابقاء على خاصية ادخال الرابط باستخدام موقع imbb :
API version 1
Imgbb's API v1 allows you to upload images.

055f593c196ff4c70d6ae063cc0e9a0c


Request method
API v1 calls can be made using the POST or GET request methods, but since GET requests are limited by the maximum allowed URL length, you should prefer the POST method.

Image Upload
https://api.imgbb.com/1/upload

Parameters
key (required)
The API key.
image (required)
A binary file, base64 data, or an image URL (up to 32 MB).
name (optional)
The name of the file; this is automatically detected if you upload a file using POST and multipart/form-data.
expiration (optional)
Enable this if you want uploads to be automatically deleted after a certain time (in seconds, 60-15552000).
Example call
curl --location --request POST "https://api.imgbb.com/1/upload?expiration=600&key=YOUR_CLIENT_API_KEY" --form "image=R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7"

Note: Always use POST when uploading local files. URL encoding may alter the base64 source due to encoded characters or simply because of the URL length limit when using GET.

API response
API v1 responses display all uploaded image information in JSON format.

In the JSON response, the headers will include status codes to let you easily determine whether the request was OK. It will also include the status property.

Example response (JSON)
{
	"data": {
		"id": "2ndCYJK",
		"title": "c1f64245afb2",
		"url_viewer": "https://ibb.co/2ndCYJK",
		"url": "https://i.ibb.co/w04Prt6/c1f64245afb2.gif",
		"display_url": "https://i.ibb.co/98W13PY/c1f64245afb2.gif",
		"width":"1",
		"height":"1",
		"size": "42",
		"time": "1552042565",
		"expiration":"0",
		"image": {
			"filename": "c1f64245afb2.gif",
			"name": "c1f64245afb2",
			"mime": "image/gif",
			"extension": "gif",
			"url": "https://i.ibb.co/w04Prt6/c1f64245afb2.gif",
		},
		"thumb": {
			"filename": "c1f64245afb2.gif",
			"name": "c1f64245afb2",
			"mime": "image/gif",
			"extension": "gif",
			"url": "https://i.ibb.co/2ndCYJK/c1f64245afb2.gif",
		},
		"medium": {
			"filename": "c1f64245afb2.gif",
			"name": "c1f64245afb2",
			"mime": "image/gif",
			"extension": "gif",
			"url": "https://i.ibb.co/98W13PY/c1f64245afb2.gif",
		},
		"delete_url": "https://ibb.co/2ndCYJK/670a7e48ddcb85ac340c717a41047e5c"
	},
	"success": true,
	"status": 200
}

**Exploring Upload Functionality**

I'm currently focused on analyzing the request. The main goal is to add image uploading via the ImgBB API, but I need to ensure the existing URL input functionality stays intact. I'm carefully considering the current codebase to understand how best to integrate this new feature without disrupting the user experience.


**Identifying Integration Points**

I've determined key areas for integration, focusing on the "Admin Dashboard" as the central hub for image management. The plan includes a utility function for ImgBB API calls, taking a `File` and returning a URL. I'm focusing on the "Add Meal" section, which currently lacks modal implementation in the provided code, to integrate this feature smoothly.


**Refining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now revising my plan for integrating image uploads. Focusing on the user-facing impact, I'm considering existing modal functionalities like "Add Plan" and "Content Management", both currently using URL inputs. The "Add/Edit Meal" modal in the `Store` tab is absent, so I'll create a reusable "Upload or URL" component to improve image input throughout the app. Furthermore, I'll establish a `imageService.ts` to manage ImgBB API calls with the necessary functions, endpoint, and key.


**Constructing New Image Component**

I've outlined a new `ImageUploader` component, focusing on the core functionality. It takes a current URL and an `onChange` callback for updates, along with a placeholder. I'm building it with both URL input and a file upload mechanism with a loading indicator and image preview. I'll integrate this by updating `AdminDashboard.tsx`, replacing the old manual inputs for image URLs within various existing modals.


**Designing Meal Upload Flow**

I'm now thinking specifically about how to incorporate image uploading into the "Add Meal" process. I'll need to add a `showMealModal` state and construct a modal similar to the existing "Add Plan" modal for this purpose. I'll be sure to adhere to the implementation plan, too.


**Integrating Meal Modal Logic**

I'm now integrating the meal modal logic. The `AdminDashboard.tsx` component requires state for `showMealModal` and the `newMeal` object. I will connect the existing "Add Meal" button to trigger the modal, and replace the old image URL input with the new `<ImageUploader>` component, ensuring it calls the parent `onChange` with the image URL on success. This update addresses the key user requirement.


**Polishing Implementation Details**

I'm now focusing on refining the implementation, paying close attention to user experience and overall structure. Specifically, I'm updating `handleSaveContent` to handle image uploads for Hero Image and App Banner. I'm also ensuring the "Add Plan Modal" and "Add Meal Modal" correctly integrate the image upload functionality. I'm also making sure to adhere to all established constraints, including language, styling, and API integration. While creating the XML, I'm making sure to handle the loading state so that users are not able to submit while uploading. I am also making sure that `ImageUploader` component handles both the file upload and URL input options to maintain a clean UI. The `AdminDashboard` will correctly invoke the `dataService` to save meal information.


**Generating Arabic UI Strings**

I'm translating the key UI strings into Arabic to match the app's tone and create a more user-friendly experience. I've translated "Upload Image" to "رفع صورة," "Image URL" to "رابط الصورة," and "Uploading..." to "جاري الرفع...". Now, I'm ready to generate the XML.

لقد قمت بتصميم وتنفيذ ميزة رفع الصور باستخدام ImgBB API. قمت بإنشاء مكون جديد `ImageUploader` يدمج بين إمكانية لصق الرابط مباشرة أو رفع ملف من الجهاز، وقمت بتحديث لوحة التحكم (AdminDashboard) لاستخدام هذا المكون في إدارة المحتوى، إضافة الخطط، **وأضفت ميزة إضافة الوجبات** التي كانت زرًا غير مفعل سابقاً لتكتمل الفائدة.

إليك التغييرات المطلوبة:

entries.description: Create a service to handle image uploads to ImgBB API.
Create a reusable component for uploading images or entering URLs.
Integrate ImageUploader, add missing Add Meal Modal, and wire up Meal creation.

payload.text: قم بتفعيل زر تعديل للوجبات المدخلة بحيث يمكن التعديل والحفظ

**Enabling the Edit Button**

I've been analyzing the request to enable the "Edit" button for meals within the Admin Dashboard. The current implementation in `AdminDashboard.tsx` shows meals and an Edit button. My focus is on determining what changes are needed to make the button active. I'm exploring the codebase now to trace how the meal data is handled and how button functionality is implemented elsewhere.


**Analyzing Edit Functionality**

I'm now diving into the implementation details for the "Edit" button, focusing on how to make it functional. My current focus is implementing the `onClick` handler and associated logic. My analysis now highlights that the button needs to open a modal, pre-filled with the selected meal's data. I also need to update the save function to differentiate between adding a new meal and updating an existing one, based on the presence of an ID. I plan to reuse the existing `Add Meal` modal design.


**Defining Edit Actions**

I've outlined the necessary steps to implement the "Edit" feature. This focuses on updating `AdminDashboard.tsx`, including managing the `newMeal` state for prepopulating edit fields. I will create a `handleEditMeal` function that copies the meal data to state, along with modified `mealIngredientsText` and `mealInstructionsText` fields. The current `handleSaveMeal` will be updated to distinguish updates from new meal additions. The function now determines the meal's ID, to then update the existing document in Firestore with the correct ID.


**Attaching Edit Functionality**

I've attached the `handleEditMeal` function to the "Edit" button's `onClick` event, integrating the direct trigger for meal data transfer. Now, I'm working on clearing the state variables, focusing on resetting the variables upon clicking the "Add Meal" button to prevent old data persisting. I've drafted the `handleEditMeal` function and the modified `handleSaveMeal` to include the ID check, including implementing state resets.


**Polishing the Modal Design**

I'm now refining the UI for the edit meal modal and `AdminDashboard.tsx`. I have finished setting up the state resets to clear form elements when opening the "Add Meal" form. I'm focusing on the modal content and UI elements to offer the optimal user experience. I'll need to modify the title based on whether the modal is for adding or editing a meal, and similarly, the submit button text. Now the focus is on finishing these modal text changes and integrating the modifications with the already-established design.


**Completing UI Integration**

I'm making the last steps, addressing all final UI components. I'm dynamically updating the modal title and button text based on the mode. I've successfully incorporated state resets to guarantee clean forms. I've finished the UI modifications and state checks to make sure the app correctly updates meals in both add and edit scenarios. Now, I'm working on the simulated code review to make sure everything works.

لقد قمت بتفعيل زر التعديل. التغييرات شملت تحديث منطق `handleSaveMeal` للتحقق مما إذا كانت الوجبة موجودة مسبقاً (تحديث) أو جديدة (إنشاء)، وإضافة دالة `handleEditMeal` لتعبئة النموذج بالبيانات الحالية عند الضغط على زر التعديل.

إليك التغييرات المطلوبة في ملف `components/AdminDashboard.tsx`:

entries.description: Enable Edit functionality for meals by adding handleEditMeal and updating handleSaveMeal logic to handle updates vs inserts.
